title: Inconsistent RepoTags and RepoDigests for manifests on podman image inspect / podman image ls and API.
body:
### Issue Description

Inconsistency for RepoTags and RepoDigests across json outputs.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Build a manifest `podman build --platform linux/arm64,linux/amd64 --manifest testmanifest .`
2. `podman image inspect testmanifest`
3. `podman image ls testmanifest --format json`
4. `curl --unix-socket /var/folders/cn/pm5mgrcs5h339nphypxtvd080000gn/T/podman/podman-machine-default-api.sock -X GET \
     "http://d/v4.2.0/libpod/images/json" | jq`
     
 


### Describe the results you received

All three sections have different ways of showing RepoTags and RepoDigests for manifests and they are inconsistent..

For example:

```
podman image inspect testmanifest
```

Shows:

```json
          "RepoTags": [],
          "RepoDigests": [],
```

```
podman image ls testmanifest --format json
```

Shows:

```json
        "RepoTags": null,
        "RepoDigests": [
            "localhost/testmanifest@sha256:1156362de6c3c112eaafcf75a8d331c868f8a61b0c9ef52ed34b8f98f415818c",
            "localhost/testmanifest@sha256:20b959ad5960230b65a77b746bdbf5d991ade4d7a129c2554e167acdcc990531"
        ],
```

And then the API with CURL:

```
curl --unix-socket /var/folders/cn/pm5mgrcs5h339nphypxtvd080000gn/T/podman/podman-machine-default-api.sock -X GET \
     "http://d/v4.2.0/libpod/images/json" | jq
```

Shows:

```
    "RepoTags": [
      "localhost/testmanifest:latest"
    ],
    "RepoDigests": [
      "localhost/testmanifest@sha256:1156362de6c3c112eaafcf75a8d331c868f8a61b0c9ef52ed34b8f98f415818c",
      "localhost/testmanifest@sha256:20b959ad5960230b65a77b746bdbf5d991ade4d7a129c2554e167acdcc990531"
    ],
 ```


### Describe the results you expected

Consistency throughout json outputs for inspect / api / image ls

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body: I'm not dead sure this is a bug.  There is not a contract between the restful endpoints and --format JSON to be exact.  All --format JSON is supposed to mean is give me the results in JSON.  In the case of docker, this might be more true because it is always interacting with its API endpoints for every command.  We do not.  I'll be curious to see what others say.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
